vulnerability.reason: Generated describes educators (and students) lacking domain expertise to detect subtle errors, which semantically matches the reference's educators lacking training to identify inaccuracies.
vulnerability.reference: Educators lack training to identify subtle AI-generated historical or scientific inaccuracies in video.
vulnerability.generated: Educators and students lack the domain expertise to detect subtle factual errors in photorealistic generated videos, trusting them as accurate.

impact.reason: Both state the negative outcome is misconceptions in science and history propagating among learners/students, damaging their knowledge base.
impact.reference: Systematic reinforcement of historical misconceptions and scientific errors in student knowledge bases.
impact.generated: Misconceptions in science and history propagate among learners, undermining educational outcomes and spreading misinformation.

dose_response.reason: Both causal arcs follow: decoder-only transformer outputs misaligned video, which users unable to detect errors adopt, leading to institutionalized/propagated misconceptions in students.
dose_response.reference: Decoder-only transformers generate misaligned educational videos from spurious correlations, which, when adopted by educators lacking verification training, institutionalizes false knowledge in students.
dose_response.generated: Decoder-only transformer generating physically implausible or semantically misaligned video, through educators and students using AI-generated video as instructional material, triggers their inability to detect factual errors, leading to propagation of misconceptions in science and history, undermining education and spreading misinformation.
